---
title: "Linear Multiple Regression + Spatial structure"
author: "Pauline Depoortere"
date: "02/12/2022"
output: pdf_document
---

# Reproducibility note

**This version retains all plots, including the extreme (outlier) values of stem density and AGB that are trimmed in the main analysis (upper/lower 3% for stem density and 2% for AGB).**

This R Markdown documents the full spatial analysis for the associated publication. **It is provided partly for information only.** Everything from here up to the `Export` section  -  the construction of the spatial weighting matrices (SWMs) and the selection of the Moran's Eigenvector Maps (MEMs)  -  relies on the exact plot coordinates (`LON`, `LAT`), which cannot be shared (see the Data Availability Statement). That part is therefore **not reproducible from the shared dataset** and is included for transparency only.

The intermediate outputs of that step, the selected MEMs, are provided directly as the `bestMEM_*` tables. **From the `Importation of MEMs` section onward, the code is fully reproducible** using those provided tables.

The input dataset (Pantropical_dataset_2023plots_V2_N_55_1_SOIL_AVECOUTLIERS.csv) is still provided in this repository so that the variables and their metadata can be
inspected.
#----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

#2. Spatial structure

```{r}
#uniqueN(data, by="PLT_trois_bis")
```

```{r}
#data %>%

#  theme_classic()
```

##2.1.Generation of a set of SWMs 

... from which the optimisation of the selection of both the SWM and the subset of MEM variables within it will be carried out:


We will test the two most contrasting graph-based criteria of connectivity as we have a irregular design (excluding the Delaunay triangulation to avoid edge effect issues), with a Gabriel graph and a minimum spanning tree. We will test these connectivity matrices without weighting the connexions and weighting them by the linear decreasing function of distance provided in the function (the further apart, the less two sites are connected or "exchange information").


```{r}
##source("listw.candidates_modif.R")

#  .addweights <- function(nb.object, nb.dist, coord.mat, weights, style, Prefix,

#    ## using a single nb.object and several weights
```

##2.2. MEMs : Optimisation procedure (Bauman et al. 2018, Ecology)

A SWM as well as a subset of MEM variables need to be selected at this stage.
As discussed in Bauman et al. (2018, Ecography), the univariate / multivariate nature of the 
response data as well as the type of question / objective should guide the choice of the type of 
spatial eigenvector selection method.

###a) MIR : Minimum number of components

In the present case, we will use the MIR criterion (see Ecography paper), hence favoring the minimisation of the model residual spatial autocorrelation using the smallest subset of spatial predictors possible.

#----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

#!!! HERE !!! Importation of MEMs

###Datatable preparation

```{r include=FALSE}

rm(list=ls()) 

#--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# CHANGING THE CLIMATE SOURCE
# 
# To produce the results for another climate source, change the 4 lines below and re-run the block. 
# Available sources: WC | CHELSA | ERA5 | CRU | TC | MODIS_CHIRPS | MODIS_IMERG
# You need to repeat once per source to get all 7.
#--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Source of EACH climate column. For most sources the three are identical to
# clim_source. For the MODIS combinations the temperature (Bio1) and the
# precipitation (Bio12/Bio15) come from different products, so they differ from
# the file tag, e.g.:
#   MODIS_IMERG  ->  clim_source="MODIS_IMERG", src_bio1="MODIS", src_bio12=src_bio15="IMERG"
#   MODIS_CHIRPS ->  clim_source="MODIS_CHIRPS", src_bio1="MODIS", src_bio12=src_bio15="CHIRPS"

clim_source <- "WC"
src_bio1  <- "WC"   # temperature   (Bio1)
src_bio12 <- "WC"   # precipitation (Bio12)
src_bio15 <- "WC"   # precipitation (Bio15)

# Set this to the folder on your own machine that contains the data file (7_Modeles_*) and where outputs will be written
 
path <- "path/to/your/folder/7_Modele_AGB_sans_montane_AVECOUTLIERS/"

setwd(path)
#--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------



#Libraries
library(data.table) #is used to import and manipulate tables
library(broom)
library(ggplot2)
#library(dotwhisker) 
library(dplyr)

library(adespatial) 
library(spdep)
library(vegan)
library(textshape) 

library(janitor)

#Set the seed (pseudo-random number generator), for exact reproducibility
set.seed(2023)

#Load the data
data<-fread("Pantropical_dataset_2023plots_V2_N_55_1_SOIL_AVECOUTLIERS.csv")

#Units : I have to multiply the Bio 15 variable by 100 (even if we are not sure for MERRACLIM)
#data[,Bio15_m:=Bio15_m*100,]

summary(data)

#Enlever les parcelles > 1000 m

data<-data[!elevation.y>=1000]
data<-data[!is.na(get(paste0("Bio1_", src_bio1)))]
```

### Choice of the data climate source

```{r}
brut=data
data=data[,.(PLT_trois_bis,Ps,Continent,Year,DBHqm,BA,SD,WD,WDba,AGB_Chave_eq7,AGB_Feld_Pan,AGB_Feld_Cont,AGB_Feld_Reg,sand,org,ph,slope,Bio1=get(paste0("Bio1_",src_bio1)),Bio12=get(paste0("Bio12_",src_bio12)),Bio15=get(paste0("Bio15_",src_bio15)))]


data[,DBHqm2:=DBHqm]
data[,BA2:=BA]
data[,SD2:=SD]
data[,WDba2:=WDba]

#data[,WDba2:=as.vector(scale(WDba, center=TRUE, scale=TRUE)),]
#data[,DBHqm2:=as.vector(scale(DBHqm, center=TRUE, scale=TRUE)),]
#data[,SD2:=as.vector(scale(SD, center=TRUE, scale=TRUE)),]
#data[,BA2:=as.vector(scale(BA, center=TRUE, scale=TRUE)),]
#data[,AGB_Feld_Pan:=as.vector(scale(AGB_Feld_Pan, center=TRUE, scale=TRUE)),]
#data[,AGB_Feld_Reg:=as.vector(scale(AGB_Feld_Reg, center=TRUE, scale=TRUE)),]
#data[,AGB_Feld_Cont:=as.vector(scale(AGB_Feld_Cont, center=TRUE, scale=TRUE)),]

## Africa
data_Af<-data[Continent=="Africa",]
class(data_Af)
## Asia
data_As<-data[Continent=="Eurasia"]
##South America 
data_SA<-data[Continent=="South America"]
##Oceania
data_Oc<-data[Continent=="Oceania"]
##North America 
data_NA<-data[Continent=="North America"]


##AGB


##Pantropic
data_AGB_Cont<-data[!is.na(AGB_Feld_Cont)]
##Africa
data_Af_AGB_Cont<-data_AGB_Cont[Continent=="Africa",]
class(data_Af)
##Asia
data_As_AGB_Cont<-data_AGB_Cont[Continent=="Eurasia"]
##South America 
data_SA_AGB_Cont<-data_AGB_Cont[Continent=="South America"]
##Oceania
data_Oc_AGB_Cont<-data_AGB_Cont[Continent=="Oceania"]
##North America 
data_NA_AGB_Cont<-data_AGB_Cont[Continent=="North America"]


data_AGB_Reg<-data[!is.na(AGB_Feld_Reg)]

## Africa
data_Af_AGB_Reg<-data_AGB_Reg[Continent=="Africa",]
class(data_Af)
## Asia
data_As_AGB_Reg<-data_AGB_Reg[Continent=="Eurasia"]
##South America 
data_SA_AGB_Reg<-data_AGB_Reg[Continent=="South America"]
##Oceania
data_Oc_AGB_Reg<-data_AGB_Reg[Continent=="Oceania"]
##North America 
data_NA_AGB_Reg<-data_AGB_Reg[Continent=="North America"]

rm(data)
rm(data_AGB_Reg)
rm(data_AGB_Cont)

```

##Standardization of all predictor and/or response variables

```{r include=FALSE}

##Africa
data_Af[,Bio1:=as.vector(scale(Bio1, center=TRUE, scale=TRUE)),]
data_Af[,Bio15:=as.vector(scale(Bio15, center=TRUE, scale=TRUE)),]
data_Af[,Bio12:=as.vector(scale(Bio12, center=TRUE, scale=TRUE)),]

data_Af[,sand:=as.vector(scale(sand, center=TRUE, scale=TRUE)),]
data_Af[,org:=as.vector(scale(org, center=TRUE, scale=TRUE)),]
data_Af[,ph:=as.vector(scale(ph, center=TRUE, scale=TRUE)),]

data_SA[,BA:=as.vector(scale(BA, center=TRUE, scale=TRUE)),]
```

#3. Multiple Linear Regression 

##3.1. DBHqm

###a) MLR Model with MEMs

```{r include=FALSE}

##Africa

DBHqmAf_F=lm(DBHqm2~., data=bestMEM_DBHqmAf)
summary(DBHqmAf_F)

#rm(bestMEM_DBHqmAf)

rse<-summary(DBHqmAf_F)$sigma
r_squared<-summary(DBHqmAf_F)$r.squared
mean<-data_Af[,mean(DBHqm2),]
results<-tidy(DBHqmAf_F, conf.int = TRUE)

setDT(results)


results<-as.data.table(t(results[2:9][p.value>=0.05,estimate:=NA]))

results<-row_to_names(results,row_number=1)
results[,`:=`(rep="SC",sub_rep="DBH_qm",area="africa",r_squared=r_squared,RMSE=(rse/mean)*100,est=NA)]
results$est<-as.character(results$est)
results[1,est:="estimate"]
results[2,est:="std.error"]
results[3,est:="statistic"]
results[4,est:="p.value"]
results[5,est:="conf.low"]
results[6,est:="conf.high"]
end_dbh<-rbind(results[,.(rep, sub_rep, area, SD ,Bio1, Bio12, Bio15, slope, sand, ph, org,r_squared,RMSE,est),])

rm(rse)
rm(mean)
rm(r_squared)
rm(results)

##South-America

DBHqmSA_F=lm(DBHqm2~., data=bestMEM_DBHqmSA)
summary(DBHqmSA_F)
#rm(bestMEM_DBHqmSA)

rse<-summary(DBHqmSA_F)$sigma
r_squared<-summary(DBHqmSA_F)$r.squared
mean<-data_SA[,mean(DBHqm2),]
results<-tidy(DBHqmSA_F, conf.int = TRUE)

setDT(results)
results<-as.data.table(t(results[2:9][p.value>=0.05,estimate:=NA]))

results<-row_to_names(results,row_number=1)
results[,`:=`(rep="SC",sub_rep="DBH_qm",area="south_america",r_squared=r_squared,RMSE=(rse/mean)*100,est=NA)]
results$est<-as.character(results$est)
results[1,est:="estimate"]
results[2,est:="std.error"]
results[3,est:="statistic"]
results[4,est:="p.value"]
results[5,est:="conf.low"]
results[6,est:="conf.high"]
end_dbh<-rbind(end_dbh,results[,.(rep, sub_rep, area, SD, Bio1, Bio12, Bio15, slope, sand, ph, org,r_squared,RMSE,est),])

rm(rse)
rm(mean)
rm(r_squared)
rm(results)



##Asia 

DBHqmAs_F=lm(DBHqm2~., data=bestMEM_DBHqmAs)
summary(DBHqmAs_F)
#rm(bestMEM_DBHqmAs)

rse<-summary(DBHqmAs_F)$sigma
r_squared<-summary(DBHqmAs_F)$r.squared
mean<-data_As[,mean(DBHqm2),]
results<-tidy(DBHqmAs_F, conf.int = TRUE)

setDT(results)
results<-as.data.table(t(results[2:9][p.value>=0.05,estimate:=NA]))

results<-row_to_names(results,row_number=1)
results[,`:=`(rep="SC",sub_rep="DBH_qm",area="asia",r_squared=r_squared,RMSE=(rse/mean)*100,est=NA)]
results$est<-as.character(results$est)
results[1,est:="estimate"]
results[2,est:="std.error"]
results[3,est:="statistic"]
results[4,est:="p.value"]
results[5,est:="conf.low"]
results[6,est:="conf.high"]
end_dbh<-rbind(end_dbh,results[,.(rep, sub_rep, area, SD, Bio1, Bio12, Bio15, slope, sand, ph, org,r_squared,RMSE,est),])
rm(rse)
rm(mean)
rm(r_squared)
rm(results)



##Oceania

DBHqmOc_F=lm(DBHqm2~., data=bestMEM_DBHqmOc)
summary(DBHqmOc_F)
#rm(bestMEM_DBHqmOc)

rse<-summary(DBHqmOc_F)$sigma
r_squared<-summary(DBHqmOc_F)$r.squared
mean<-data_Oc[,mean(DBHqm2),]
results<-tidy(DBHqmOc_F, conf.int = TRUE)

setDT(results)
results<-as.data.table(t(results[2:9][p.value>=0.05,estimate:=NA]))

results<-row_to_names(results,row_number=1)

results[6,est:="conf.high"]
```

```{r include=FALSE}
##Asia 
```

```{r include=FALSE}
rm(AGB_Feld_PanAs_F)
```

```{r include=FALSE}
#rm(bestMEM_AGB_Feld_PanAs_sd)

r_squared<-summary(AGB_Feld_PanAs_F)$r.squared

results<-tidy(AGB_Feld_PanAs_F, conf.int = TRUE)

results[,`:=`(rep="AGB",sub_rep="AGB_Feld_Pan",area="asia",r_squared=r_squared,RMSE=(rse/mean)*100,est=NA)]
results$est<-as.character(results$est)
results[1,est:="estimate"]
results[2,est:="std.error"]
results[3,est:="statistic"]
results[4,est:="p.value"]
results[5,est:="conf.low"]
results[6,est:="conf.high"]
end_agb_sd<-rbind(end_agb_sd,results[,.(rep, sub_rep, area, SD , Bio1, Bio12, Bio15,slope, sand, ph, org,r_squared,RMSE,est),])
```

```{r include=FALSE}
end_agb_wdba<-rbind(end_agb_wdba,results[,.(rep, sub_rep, area, WDba,BA,Bio1, Bio12, Bio15, slope, sand, ph, org,r_squared,RMSE,est),])
rm(rse)
rm(mean)
rm(r_squared)
rm(results)




##South-America

rm(AGB_Feld_ContSA_F)
AGB_Feld_ContSA_F=lm(AGB_Feld_Cont~., data=bestMEM_AGB_Feld_ContSA_wdba)
summary(AGB_Feld_ContSA_F)
#rm(bestMEM_AGB_Feld_ContSA_wdba)

rse<-summary(AGB_Feld_ContSA_F)$sigma
r_squared<-summary(AGB_Feld_ContSA_F)$r.squared
mean<-data_SA_AGB_Cont[,mean(AGB_Feld_Cont),]
results<-tidy(AGB_Feld_ContSA_F, conf.int = TRUE)

setDT(results)
results<-as.data.table(t(results[2:10][p.value>=0.05,estimate:=NA]))

results<-row_to_names(results,row_number=1)
results[,`:=`(rep="AGB",sub_rep="AGB_Feld_Cont",area="south_america",r_squared=r_squared,RMSE=(rse/mean)*100,est=NA)]
results$est<-as.character(results$est)
results[1,est:="estimate"]
results[2,est:="std.error"]
results[3,est:="statistic"]
results[4,est:="p.value"]
results[5,est:="conf.low"]
results[6,est:="conf.high"]
end_agb_wdba<-rbind(end_agb_wdba,results[,.(rep, sub_rep, area,WDba,BA,Bio1, Bio12, Bio15, slope, sand, ph, org,r_squared,RMSE,est),])
rm(rse)
rm(mean)
rm(r_squared)
rm(results)
```

# Export the final tables 

```{r}
setwd(path)
end_dbh[,source:=clim_source,]
fwrite(end_dbh,paste0("end_dbh_stand_", clim_source, ".csv"))

end_wdba[,source:=clim_source,]
fwrite(end_wdba,paste0("end_wdba_stand_", clim_source, ".csv"))

end_ba[,source:=clim_source,]
fwrite(end_ba,paste0("end_ba_stand_", clim_source, ".csv"))

end_agb[,source:=clim_source,]
fwrite(end_agb,paste0("end_agb_stand_", clim_source, ".csv"))

end_agb_all[,source:=clim_source,]
fwrite(end_agb_all,paste0("end_agb_all_stand_", clim_source, ".csv"))

end_agb_dbh_sd[,source:=clim_source,]
fwrite(end_agb_dbh_sd,paste0("end_agb_dbh_sd_stand_", clim_source, ".csv"))

end_agb_sd[,source:=clim_source,]
fwrite(end_agb_sd,paste0("end_agb_sd_stand_", clim_source, ".csv"))

end_agb_wdba[,source:=clim_source,]
fwrite(end_agb_wdba,paste0("end_agb_wdba_stand_", clim_source, ".csv"))

```
